# services.matrix-continuwuity

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by scripts/nix-options.sh. Do not edit by hand. -->

All options under `services.matrix-continuwuity`.

| Option | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `services.matrix-continuwuity.admin.enable` | `boolean` | Add conduwuit command to PATH for administration |
| `services.matrix-continuwuity.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable continuwuity. |
| `services.matrix-continuwuity.extraEnvironment` | `attribute set of string` | Extra Environment variables to pass to the continuwuity server. |
| `services.matrix-continuwuity.group` | `non-empty string` | The group {command}`continuwuity` is run as. |
| `services.matrix-continuwuity.package` | `package` | The matrix-continuwuity package to use. |
| `services.matrix-continuwuity.settings` | `open submodule of (TOML value)` | Generates the continuwuity.toml configuration file. Refer to <https://continuwuity.org/configuration.html> for details on supported values. |
| `services.matrix-continuwuity.settings.global.address` | `null or (list of non-empty string)` | Addresses (IPv4 or IPv6) to listen on for connections by the reverse proxy/tls terminator. If set to `null`, continuwuity will listen on IPv4 and IPv6 localhost. Must be `null` if `unix_socket_path` is set. |
| `services.matrix-continuwuity.settings.global.allow_announcements_check` | `boolean` | If enabled, continuwuity will send a simple GET request periodically to <https://continuwuity.org/.well-known/continuwuity/announcements> for any new announcements made. |
| `services.matrix-continuwuity.settings.global.allow_encryption` | `boolean` | Whether new encrypted rooms can be created. Note: existing rooms will continue to work. |
| `services.matrix-continuwuity.settings.global.allow_federation` | `boolean` | Whether this server federates with other servers. |
| `services.matrix-continuwuity.settings.global.allow_registration` | `boolean` | Whether new users can register on this server. Registration with token requires `registration_token` or `registration_token_file` to be set. If set to true without a token configured, and `yes_i_am_very_very_sure_i_want_an_open_registration_server_prone_to_abuse` is set to true, users can freely register. |
| `services.matrix-continuwuity.settings.global.database_path` | `absolute path` | Path to the continuwuity database, the directory where continuwuity will save its data. Note that database_path cannot be edited because of the service's reliance on systemd StateDir. |
| `services.matrix-continuwuity.settings.global.max_request_size` | `positive integer, meaning >0` | Max request size in bytes. Don't forget to also change it in the proxy. |
| `services.matrix-continuwuity.settings.global.port` | `list of 16 bit unsigned integer; between 0 and 65535 (both inclusive)` | The port(s) continuwuity will be running on. You need to set up a reverse proxy in your web server (e.g. apache or nginx), so all requests to /\_matrix on port 443 and 8448 will be forwarded to the continuwuity instance running on this port. |
| `services.matrix-continuwuity.settings.global.server_name` | `non-empty string` | The server_name is the name of this server. It is used as a suffix for user and room ids. |
| `services.matrix-continuwuity.settings.global.trusted_servers` | `list of non-empty string` | Servers listed here will be used to gather public keys of other servers (notary trusted key servers). Currently, continuwuity doesn't support inbound batched key requests, so this list should only contain other Synapse servers. Example: `[ "matrix.org" "constellatory.net" "tchncs.de" ]` |
| `services.matrix-continuwuity.settings.global.unix_socket_path` | `null or absolute path` | Listen on a UNIX socket at the specified path. If listening on a UNIX socket, listening on an address will be disabled. The `address` option must be set to `null` (the default value). The option {option}`services.continuwuity.group` must be set to a group your reverse proxy is part of. This will automatically add a system user "continuwuity" to your system if {option}`services.continuwuity.user` is left at the default, and a "continuwuity" group if {option}`services.continuwuity.group` is left at the default. |
| `services.matrix-continuwuity.settings.global.unix_socket_perms` | `positive integer, meaning >0` | The default permissions (in octal) to create the UNIX socket with. |
| `services.matrix-continuwuity.user` | `non-empty string` | The user {command}`continuwuity` is run as. |
